label: Transparent waxcap mushroom motif
prompt:
Use case: photorealistic-natural
Asset type: Morphous Waxcap transparent motif chroma-key source
Primary request: Create only a waxcap mushroom motif for a design-system catalog, isolated as a single complete subject on a removable chroma-key background.
Subject: A bright unimproved meadow waxcap mushroom with a glossy scarlet-orange waxy cap, orange gills visible at the cap edge, slim amber-orange stem, and a small tuft of fresh green meadow grass at the base. Natural fungal texture, clear waxy highlights, accurate waxcap form, crisp edges, unmistakably waxcap mushroom.
Composition/framing: centered complete cutout with generous padding; show full cap, gills, stem, and grass tuft without cropping. Clean studio lighting, no cast shadow.
Chroma-key background: perfectly flat solid #ff00ff background for removal. The background must be one uniform color with no shadows, gradients, texture, reflections, floor plane, or lighting variation. Do not use #ff00ff anywhere in the subject.
Constraints: no fly agaric spots, no white dots, no bracket fungus, no forest floor, no moss carpet, no second mushroom, no insects, no hand, no props, no landscape, no text, no watermark.

label: Light-mode design-system board
prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: Morphous Waxcap light-mode design-system board
Primary request: Generate one comprehensive light-mode design-system board for a Morphous theme named "Morphous Waxcap", derived from the visible transparent waxcap mushroom cutout. This board is a source reference for implementing a shadcn/tweakcn-compatible bright field product UI theme.
Canvas: exact wide landscape 16:9 composition, 3840 x 2160 4K-class board, no portrait crop, no square crop.
Resolution and legibility: high-resolution source board, at least 2400px wide. Use large readable labels and avoid dense tiny text.
Required visible sections: Morphous Waxcap, Waxcap Mushroom, Palette, Type, Japanese Typography, Components, Navigation, Dashboard/Data, Forms, Tables, Command/Search, Status, Tokens, Texture, Radius, Spacing, Shadows.
Reference use: Extract palette, texture rhythm, contrast, spacing mood, and interaction language from the visible motif only: glossy scarlet-orange waxcap cap, vivid orange gills, amber stem, fresh green meadow grass, straw-green blade tips, pale wax highlights, and dark olive loam shadows. The board must read as waxcap mushroom in an unimproved meadow, not fly agaric or generic mushroom.
Typography: English product UI examples with Japanese typography guidance. Use labels such as Geist, Noto Sans JP, Display, Body, Label, Numbers, Japanese UI, 日本語 UI. Keep all text short and legible.
UI coverage: buttons, inputs, select, textarea, checkbox, radio, switch, tabs, badges, alerts, cards, table rows, search command palette, sidebar navigation, dashboard metric cards, line chart, bar chart, empty/loading/error states, spacing tokens, radius tokens, border tokens, shadow tokens, texture samples, simple field-system icons.
Style: premium shadcn/tweakcn-style bright field design system, precise grid, 8px radius on cards and controls, meadow-cream surfaces, scarlet waxcap primary actions, orange gill secondary states, meadow-green navigation and success, straw highlights, dark olive text and borders, subtle waxy cap sheen and meadow blade texture.
Color constraints: use only motif-derived colors and near-neutrals from the motif. No unrelated saturated colors, no generic purple or blue gradients, no one-hue palette.
Text constraints: no watermark, no random brand names, no misspelling of "Morphous Waxcap" or "Waxcap Mushroom", no tiny unreadable paragraphs.

label: Dark-mode design-system board
prompt:
Use case: ui-mockup
Asset type: Morphous Waxcap dark-mode design-system board
Primary request: Generate one comprehensive dark-mode design-system board for the Morphous theme named "Morphous Waxcap". It must feel like the visible light-mode board translated to dark mode, not a new theme, and it must remain derived from the visible transparent waxcap mushroom cutout.
Canvas: exact wide landscape 16:9 composition, 3840 x 2160 4K-class board, no portrait crop, no square crop.
Resolution and legibility: high-resolution source board, at least 2400px wide. Use large readable labels and avoid dense tiny text.
Required visible sections: Morphous Waxcap, Waxcap Mushroom, Dark Mode, Palette, Type, Japanese Typography, Components, Navigation, Dashboard/Data, Forms, Tables, Command/Search, Status, Tokens, Texture, Radius, Spacing, Shadows.
Reference use: Use the visible transparent waxcap motif and the visible light-mode Morphous Waxcap board as references. Preserve motif-derived palette roles, component language, 8px radius, meadow navigation, waxy cap texture, gill line texture, Japanese typography, dashboard/data charts, forms, tables, command/search, status states, tokens, spacing, and shadows.
Visual details: dark background derived from dark olive loam and deep meadow shadow; foreground from pale wax and straw highlights; primary actions from glossy scarlet waxcap; secondary accents from orange gills and amber stem; navigation and success from meadow green; borders from muted loam; subtle wax sheen and meadow blade texture overlays. Keep a bright-field identity even in dark mode.
Typography: English product UI examples with Japanese typography guidance. Use labels such as Geist, Noto Sans JP, Display, Body, Label, Numbers, Japanese UI, 日本語 UI. Keep all text short and legible.
UI coverage: buttons, inputs, select, textarea, checkbox, radio, switch, tabs, badges, alerts, cards, table rows, search command palette, sidebar navigation, dashboard metric cards, line chart, bar chart, empty/loading/error states, spacing tokens, radius tokens, border tokens, shadow tokens, texture samples, simple field-system icons.
Color constraints: use only motif-derived colors and near-neutrals from the motif. No neon colors, no unrelated saturated colors, no generic purple or blue gradients, no pure-black-only flat background, no one-hue palette.
Text constraints: no watermark, no random brand names, no misspelling of "Morphous Waxcap" or "Waxcap Mushroom", no tiny unreadable paragraphs.
